violations.text: The non-food contact surfaces of all the walk-in cooler fans, and RTE display case have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2026
Leak was observed in front of deli walk-in cooler. Establishment must fix leak. New Violation. Correct By: 01/30/2026

The floor in the raw meat walk-in cooler, and floor in the raw meat prep area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/29/2026

Raw animal foods (chicken) were observed stored above (ribs) in the meat display case. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025
Walk-in cooler located in the receiving area was not in good repair. Inspector observed dripping condensate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. 1) (Shelled eggs) were observed stored directly on top of (cake) in walk-in cooler. 2) (Shaved steak) were observed stored above (pot pies) in retail case. 3) (turkey) were observed stored above (beef) in retail case. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

1) Deli meats were observed held at 45-49 degrees Fahrenheit. See disposal. 2) Hot dogs and sausage were observed held at 45 degrees Fahrenheit. Per interview with PIC, cooler was stocked less than one hour prior, foods were relocated to another unit. 3) Several prepared foods were observed held at 45-47 degrees Fahrenheit. See disposal. 4) Ham was observed held at 48 degrees Fahrenheit. See disposal. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Deli meats were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Roast beef is served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025
Chemicals were stored on the same shelf with food/equipment/single service articles in the deli area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Bread packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels (soup station). Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by dead flies on glue board. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
Produce was observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Coffee bean scoops were observed stored in self-service bins of beans with handles in direct contact with product. Between uses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025

Knives are stored on an unclean surface (kitchen - in between table and wall) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

Wood is used in the construction of the scale shelf in kitchen area. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025

The following were not in good repair: 1) Walk-in cooler located in the receiving area was not in good repair. Inspector observed a temporary fix on walk-in cooler to catch drippings condensation. 2) Open Air Case (entrance), ambient 48F. 3) Open Air Case (deli meats), ambient 45F. 4) Deli Display Case, ambient 45F.Service was called at time of inspection. Equipment must be maintained in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler shelving have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2025

The floors and walls throughout the meat department have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2025
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2024

The handwashing sink in the deli area is in poor repair, inspector observed stagnant water and inadequate drainage. Hand-washing sinks must be located to allow convenient use by employees in food preparation, food dispensing, and ware-washing areas. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2024

The presence of flies is not controlled as evidenced by small flying insects. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2024
Grey water was observed stagnant in produce area. Liquid wastes to be drained to disposal per law. Specific zoned area closed during time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/30/2024

Rotisserie chickens were observed held between 124-129 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Inspector observed that chickens at front/opening of the open-faced case were held at lower temperatures than those at the rear of case. PIC disposed of chickens in pig feed bin held under 135 degrees; all chickens must be kept toward rear of case. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2024
The grocery walk-in freezer door was observed with rust and sharp, exposed edges. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the mixer has an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/30/2024

The hand sink of the 3-bay in the produce prep area is in poor repair. Inspector observed a leak at the drain connection to the sink bay. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/13/2024
The floor drain in the produce walk-in was observed with pooled water in drain. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (mixer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Turkey, chicken, and pasta were marked with a date exceeding 7 days. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Sliced tomatoes observed not marked with the date of preparation. Date marking was inconsistent on pasta, which was date-marked with 2 dates exceeding 7 days. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
Clean muffin pans were observed stored on the floor in the kitchen storage area. PIC brought to 3-bay sink to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean on a shelf beneath the prep table. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
The grocery walk-in freezer door observed with rust and sharp, exposed edges. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024
The non-food contact surfaces (under top shelving) of the rotisserie chicken display was observed with and accumulation of food and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the mixer have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
The air-gap at the 3-bay in in the produce prep area is misaligned, resulting in water leaking on floor New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
The floor drain in the produce walk-in was observed with water pooled in drain. New Violation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Litter, leaf litter, debris, and unused items observed around the refuse area. Refuse areas must be maintained clean and free of debris to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
The hand-washing sink in the deli area was lacking paper towels. Staff replaced papaer towels during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (mixer, peeler and knife on clean knife rack) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Multiple food items were observed stored on the floor in the storage/electrical room in the kitchen. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
Salad spinner observed stored on floor in storage/electrical room in kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024
Single service items observed stored on floor in the storage/electrical closet and in the produce area. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in several areas of the kitchen. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
The grocery walk-in freezer door observed with rust and sharp, exposed edges. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024
Non-food contact surface of the rotisserie chicken display case was observed with an accumulation of food and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: -stand mixer -carts Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
The air gap at the 3-bay sink in the produce area is misaligned, resulting in water leaking on floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/23/2024
The doors to the toilet rooms are not self-closing, with clean uniforms stored directly outside. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Litter, leaf litter, debris and unused items observed around the refuse area. Refuse areas must be maintained clean and free of debris to prevent harborage conditions. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The ceiling tiles near air vent in the kitchen prep area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The floors and walls (especially at the floor/wall junctures) throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored on a shelf in the produce area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Items were removed during inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/09/2024

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Inspector provided state guidance at inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

Employees were observed changing gloves without washing their hands after touching their hair and face. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

The hand-washing sink in the deli counter area was blocked by a case of cheese. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Box was moved during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

The produce prep area is lacking a hand-washing sink. One bay of the 3-bay sink used for food prep must be designated for hand washing and supplied with soap, paper towels, and proper signage. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
Washed fruit wash placed in an un-sanitized container before being placed into mix fruit containers. Items were disposed in pig feed buckets. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-200ppm. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

Ham and corned beef were observed with date marks exceeding 7 days from preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. PIC disposed in pig feed buckets. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Per PIC food is left out to cool for 3-4 hours to get down to 70 degrees. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024

Multiple food items were observed stored on the floor in the electrical closet at prep area, grocery freezer walk-in, and dairy walk-in.. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
Equipment and utensils observed drying on wet cloths during inspection. Air drying rack needed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean throughout the facility. Single service items at the deli counter were observed stored in a re-used chicken box, and open single service items were disposed of. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
Shelving in walk-in near produce area was not in good repair. Inspector obsered bricks being used to hold-up shelves. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024
The grocery walk-in freezer door observed with rust and sharp, exposed edges. New Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the following areas have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris:-open air cases throughout facility-stove-hood panels above stove-stand mixer-door tracks at deli case-gaskets on walk-in near produce area-prep tables-floors in meat area walk-in-carts-shelving throughout kitchen, including underside-wire shelving for single-service items in prep kitchen New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
The floor drain in the produce walk-in was observed with water pooled in drain. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
The 3-bay sink that is used as a prep sink is lacking an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024
The doors to the staff toilet rooms are not self-closing, with clean uniforms stored directly outside. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Litter, leaf litter, and debris and unused items observed around the refuse and receiving area. Refuse areas must be maintained clean and free of debris to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

The ceiling tiles and light fixtures in the kitchen area have an accumulation of soil residue, dust, and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen and clean work uniform area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 04/24/2024
